summary: Users & Permissions Management feature (v84.1.0) tested end-to-end. All 16 backend tests pass. Frontend UI tests pass - Users tab is first sub-tab in Settings, Add User dialog works with role selection and permission toggles, tab visibility correctly enforced based on permissions.

backend_tests.test_01_admin_login_returns_permissions: PASS - Admin login returns all permissions
backend_tests.test_04_create_user_entry_operator: PASS - Create user with entry_operator role
backend_tests.test_05_create_user_and_login: PASS - Created user can login with correct permissions
backend_tests.test_06_update_user_role_and_permissions: PASS - Update user role and permissions

frontend_tests.users_tab_is_first: PASS - Users tab is default/first sub-tab
frontend_tests.add_user_dialog: PASS - Opens with role selection and permission toggles
frontend_tests.role_selection_updates_permissions: PASS - Entry Operator: can_edit=ON, can_delete=OFF, can_see_payments=OFF
frontend_tests.tab_visibility_payments: PASS - Payments tab hidden for entry_operator
frontend_tests.tab_visibility_cashbook: PASS - CashBook tab hidden for entry_operator
frontend_tests.tab_visibility_reports: PASS - Reports tab hidden for entry_operator
frontend_tests.tab_visibility_settings: PASS - Settings tab hidden for entry_operator

critical_code_review_comments: Backend converts usernames to lowercase on create - this is correct behavior
ROLE_PERMISSIONS in auth.py matches ROLE_DEFAULTS in Settings.jsx - good consistency
Tab visibility in TabNavigation.jsx correctly filters based on user.permissions
seed_data_creation: Test users created and deactivated during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Users & Permissions feature is fully implemented. Users tab is first sub-tab in Settings. Default users are 'admin' (admin role) and 'staff' (staff role). Role selection auto-updates permission toggles. Permission-based tab visibility is enforced in TabNavigation.jsx. Test users with prefix 'test_' are created and deactivated during testing.
